reasoning: The explosion VFX spans roughly 80-90px in the scene while the player entity (dark blob on platform) occupies approximately 50-60px wide. This puts the explosion at ~1.5-1.6x the player footprint, which for a momentary impact effect is borderline — explosion VFX may legitimately exceed player size, but ideally a single-hit blast should stay within 1.0-1.5x player to preserve toy-diorama feel. The burst does not overwhelm the scene (scene canvas is ~1000px wide, explosion is ~9% of width), and the pixel-art style, color palette, and top-down perspective all match the game aesthetic correctly. No perspective inconsistency; the flat-sprite explosion sits flush on the ground plane consistent with other elements. Overall the effect reads as a satisfying hit without dominating the screen, but it sits at the upper edge of acceptable scale for a category-1 explosion.
